rows.locks: Eligible Godot source text imports declarative scene nodes, presentation resources, input actions, provenance, and fidelity records into neutral IR without a Godot runtime bridge or trusted Studio writes.
Unsupported Godot nodes, script references, signal connections, and other lossy or behavior-bearing touchpoints are explicit Yellow/Red gaps and must never be silently graded clean/Green.
The demo report validator rejects claimed_ported_units, so an ungated or auto-translated port claim fails until later Ouroforge-native oracle evidence passes.
The canonical Godot migration IR state-hash is stable for identical source text and changes when declarative source data changes, catching nondeterministic or stale-hash regressions.

Coverage v78 records the one-way on-ramp, source-project/open-text legal boundary, clean-room re-derivation hand-off, Rust data-plane ownership, no Studio trusted writes, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v78 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era O M89 Godot 2D adapter to IR. It locks source-project/open-text .tscn/.tres/project.godot parsing, one-way import to Ouroforge-native skeleton IR, clean-room re-derivation touchpoints, no Godot runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gaps for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash behavior, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
